key_changes: Updated jj-gitignore-audit subtask status from pending to completed
Updated bop-serve subtask status from pending to completed
Added notes field to both subtasks documenting implementation details
Added updated_at timestamps for audit trail

patterns_discovered.description: Team-specific Unicode glyphs used in card directory names for visual identification
patterns_discovered.example: 🂲 for team-arch, 🂢 for team-cli in .cards/pending/ paths

patterns_discovered.description: Cards verified using cargo run with jq filtering to confirm indexing
patterns_discovered.example: cargo run --bin bop -- list --state pending --json | jq -r '.[] | select(.id == "bop-serve") | .id'

patterns_discovered.description: Subtasks tracked with status, spec_content, notes, and updated_at for complete audit trail

gotchas_discovered.description: Cards created in team-specific pending directories with glyph prefixes, not generic .cards/pending/
gotchas_discovered.impact: Card path includes emoji characters which must be handled correctly in filesystem operations

gotchas_discovered.description: ISO 8601 timestamps with microseconds and timezone offset required for audit trail
Notes field documents implementation decisions, command invocations, and verification steps - critical for reproducibility
gotchas_discovered.content: Detailed notes capture how cards were created, what spec was added, and how verification was performed

outcome_details.verification_method: Card indexed correctly, queryable via bop list with id=bop-serve

recommendations.recommendation: Continue documenting implementation decisions in notes field for full audit trail and future reference
Maintain glyph-based team identification in card names for visual card categorization across multiple teams
Always verify card indexing after creation using cargo bop list command to ensure proper registration
Use consistent ISO 8601 format with timezone for all implementation plan timestamps to maintain chronological integrity
Include detailed requirements, scope boundaries (IN/OUT), and explicit acceptance criteria in card specs for clarity
